title: Gymnopteris phanerodictyon
taxonomy: Gymnopteris phanerodictyon is a species of fern in the family Matoniaceae. The classification of Gymnopteris phanerodictyon places it in the phylum Pteridophyta and the class Polypodiopsida. Further classification includes the subclass Osmundineae and the order Matoniales. Within the family Matoniaceae, Gymnopteris phanerodictyon is one of the several species that are native to the tropical regions of Asia. The classification of Gymnopteris phanerodictyon highlights its unique position within the fern family.

intro: Gymnopteris phanerodictyon, also known as the Indian maidenhair fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Matoniaceae. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Gymnopteris phanerodictyon include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Gymnopteris phanerodictyon is commonly used to make decoctions and infusions, as well as being incorporated into various Ayurvedic formulations, such as herbal teas and tonics, to promote health and well-being.
